DragonNet — shared representation and three heads (torch)

Source: R/causalDeepNet.R
dragonnet.Rd

DragonNet uses a shared representation and three heads: propensity \(\hat{e}(X)\), \(\hat{Y}(0)\), and \(\hat{Y}(1)\), with optional targeted regularization (learnable \(\varepsilon\)). Architecture follows [causalml dragonnet.py](https://github.com/uber/causalml/blob/master/causalml/inference/tf/dragonnet.py): 3 shared layers (ELU), propensity head (sigmoid), and two outcome branches (2 hidden layers each, then output).

Usage

dragonnet(
  X,
  treatment,
  y,
  neurons = 200L,
  reg_l2 = 0.01,
  targeted_reg = TRUE,
  ratio_tar = 1,
  batch_size = 64L,
  val_split = 0.2,
  adam_epochs = 30L,
  adam_lr = 0.001,
  sgd_epochs = 100L,
  sgd_lr = 1e-05,
  sgd_momentum = 0.9,
  verbose = TRUE,
  ...
)

Arguments

X

covariate matrix or data.frame

treatment

binary treatment 0/1

y

outcome vector

neurons

units per shared layer (default 200)

reg_l2

L2 regularization for outcome heads (default 0.01)

targeted_reg

if TRUE (default), use targeted regularization in the loss

ratio_tar

weight for targeted regularization term (default 1)

batch_size

batch size for training (default 64)

val_split

fraction for validation (default 0.2)

adam_epochs

epochs for Adam phase (default 30)

adam_lr

Adam learning rate (default 1e-3)

sgd_epochs

epochs for SGD phase (default 100)

sgd_lr

SGD learning rate (default 1e-5)

sgd_momentum

SGD momentum (default 0.9)

verbose

if TRUE, print epoch loss

...

ignored

Value

Object of class dragonnet (torch model when available, else placeholder) with predict() for ITE and optional propensity.

Details

When torch is installed, the full DragonNet is fitted (Adam then SGD). Otherwise falls back to an nnet or ranger placeholder.

References

Shi, C., Blei, D., Veitch, V. (2019). Adapting Neural Networks for the Estimation of Treatment Effects. https://arxiv.org/abs/1906.02120.

https://github.com/uber/causalml/blob/master/causalml/inference/tf/dragonnet.py

See also

RCausalML-package

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Basic usage
# dragonnet(...)
} # }
